title: Elatostema longeacuminatum
intro: Elatostema longeacuminatum, also known as Long-Leaf Elatostema, is a species of flowering plant in the family Urticaceae, native to the tropical regions of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe various ailments such as skin irritations and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Elatostema longeacuminatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often consumed to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
taxonomy: Elatostema longeacuminatum is a species of flowering plant in the nettle family Urticaceae. This plant is classified as part of the genus Elatostema, which comprises over 200 species of perennial plants. Elatostema longeacuminatum is native to Asia, particularly in China, and is known for its unique, elongated leaf shape. The exact classification of Elatostema longeacuminatum is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Rosids, Order: Rosales, Family: Urticaceae, Genus: Elatostema, Species: E. longeacuminatum.
